document.title: Risk Management Plan for B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management plan for H&abyz Co., Ltd. B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models B1417MCW, B1717MCW, FB1417MCW, and FB1717MCW.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Establishes and documents the risk management plan, responsibilities, risk acceptability criteria, verification activities, and production/post-production information review activities.
keyPoints: The document applies to all stages of the life cycle of the listed detector models.
The product is classified as IIa and has an expected service life of approximately 5 years.
Risk acceptability uses a severity multiplied by probability matrix with risk levels 1 to 4 acceptable and 5 to 25 unacceptable.
Overall residual risk is evaluated in relation to the benefits of the intended use after risk control measures have been implemented and verified.
Production and post-production information is collected from internal and external sources, including complaints, PMCF, CAPA, incidents, literature, and similar device information.

concepts.name: Risk management plan
concepts.evidence: This is a document for the H&abyz Co., Ltd. to establish and document a risk management plan in accordance with the risk management procedure.

concepts.name: Risk acceptability criteria
concepts.evidence: We define and document the policy for determining criteria for risk acceptability;

concepts.name: Overall residual risk evaluation
concepts.evidence: After all risk control measures have been implemented and verified, the manufacturer shall evaluate the overall residual risk posed by the medical device

concepts.name: Production and post-production information
concepts.evidence: The get information method of post-production information should be collected according to the internal information and the external information.

concepts.name: Essential performance
concepts.evidence: Manufacturer shall identify which functions are Essential Performance.

concepts.name: Intended purpose
concepts.evidence: The B1417MCW/B1717MCW/FB1417MCW/FB1717MCW is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: The document is a risk management plan for the B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Management Plan for B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The document covers models B1417MCW, B1717MCW, FB1417MCW, and FB1717MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Model name: B1417MCW/B1717MCW/FB1417MCW/FB1717MCW

evidence.claim: The document was prepared, reviewed, and approved on Oct 18, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared by Kim, Myeong seop R&D team Oct 18, 2024 Reviewed by Lee, Dong ho R&D Manager Oct 18, 2024 Approved by Hur, Nam kyu Quality Manager Oct 18, 2024

evidence.claim: Revision 0 was established on Oct/18/2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Revision No. 0 Rev. Date Oct/18/2024 Revision Description Establishment

evidence.claim: The plan applies to all stages of the product life cycle.
evidence.evidenceText: This Risk management plan is applicable to all stages of the life cycle of the B1417MCW/B17MCW/FB1417MCW/FB1717MCW.

evidence.claim: The product classification is IIa.
evidence.evidenceText: Classification IIa

evidence.claim: The expected service life is approximately 5 years.
evidence.evidenceText: Expected service life Approximately 5 years

evidence.claim: The detector is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: The B1417MCW/B1717MCW/FB1417MCW/FB1717MCW is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: The detector is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnosis and treatment planning on a real time basis for physicians and radiologists.

evidence.claim: The applicable European regulation identified is Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
evidence.evidenceText: European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 5 April 2017

evidence.claim: EN ISO 14971:2019 is listed as a relevant standard.
evidence.evidenceText: EN ISO 14971:2019 Medical devices - Application of risk management to medical devices (ISO 14971:2019)

evidence.claim: Risk acceptability criteria are based on applicable national or regional regulations and relevant international standards.
evidence.evidenceText: This policy shall ensure that criteria are based upon applicable national or regional regulations and relevant International Standards

evidence.claim: Risk level is calculated by multiplying severity rating by probability rating.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk level = Severity rating × Probability rating

evidence.claim: Risk levels 1 to 4 are acceptable and risk levels 5 to 25 are unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Level: 1~4 Green, Acceptable Risk (A) Acceptable region Risk Level: 5~25 Red, Unacceptable Risk (U) Unacceptable region

evidence.claim: Unacceptable risk policy is project cancellation or device redesign.
evidence.evidenceText: Unacceptable Risk (U) Unacceptable risk Project cancellation or device redesign

evidence.claim: Overall residual risk is evaluated after risk control measures have been implemented and verified.
evidence.evidenceText: After all risk control measures have been implemented and verified, the manufacturer shall evaluate the overall residual risk posed by the medical device

evidence.claim: If overall residual risk is acceptable, users are informed of significant residual risks in accompanying documentation.
evidence.evidenceText: If the overall residual risk is judged acceptable, the manufacturer shall inform users of significant residual risks and shall include the necessary information in the accompanying documentation

evidence.claim: Internal post-production information sources include PMCF, CAPA reports, customer complaints, service records, and customer satisfaction surveys.
evidence.evidenceText: Internal (1) PMCF (Post Market Clinical Follow up) (2) Corrective and preventive actions related reports (3) Customer Complaints (4) A / S recorded and maintained the information obtained during maintenance (5) Customer satisfaction surveys

evidence.claim: External post-production information sources include medical incident reports, academic papers, expert opinions, similar device information, laws and standards trends, and clinical trial information.
evidence.evidenceText: External (1) Reporting medical incidents (Recall, the side effects reported cases of domestic and foreign) (2) Related academic papers and expert opinions (3) Information related similar medical device (4) Establishment and revised trends in international and domestic laws / standards (5) In the process of performing clinical trials information

evidence.claim: The Quality manager reports safety information and side effects to the regulatory agency when necessary.
evidence.evidenceText: According to the seriousness of the information collected, the Quality manager shall report safety information and any side effects to the regulatory agency (Applicable national or regional regulations).

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the source text identifies European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 and Korean Medical Device Act references; FDA is not supported by the provided source text.
Model list contains an apparent inconsistency: most occurrences state B1717MCW, but the life-cycle scope sentence states B17MCW.
No source page numbers were provided in the preprocessed text.
